title: Heston Stochastic Volatility Calibration
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Heston Stochastic Volatility Calibration: Heston model calibration: fit 5-parameter stochastic vol model to observed implied volatility surface. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.heston.characteristic_function…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; data_sparsity_OTM_options, term_structure_inversion_error set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: dS = rS dt + sqrt(v) S dW_S; dv = kappa(theta-v)dt + xi*sqrt(v)*dW_v; option price via Fourier inversion
E.world_state_x: Heston parameters (v0, kappa, theta, xi, rho) describing vol dynamics
E.observation_y: implied vol surface sigma_impl(K, T) or market prices C(K_i, T_j)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered stochastic_vol_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); data_sparsity_OTM_options dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Market bid ask gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: vol surface RMSE < 50 bp for liquid options. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |data_sparsity_OTM_options|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_options * N_FFT_points) per forward evaluation per iteration
